      35              : typedef enum

      37              :         ETCS_NEEDS_REBUILD,

      39              :         ETCS_VALID
      40              : } EventTriggerCacheStateType;
      41              : 
      42              : typedef struct
      43              : {
      44              :         EventTriggerEvent event;
      45              :         List       *triggerlist;
      46              : } EventTriggerCacheEntry;
      47              : 
      48              : static HTAB *EventTriggerCache;
      49              : static MemoryContext EventTriggerCacheContext;
      50              : static EventTriggerCacheStateType EventTriggerCacheState = ETCS_NEEDS_REBUILD;
      51              : 
      52              : static void BuildEventTriggerCache(void);
      53              : static void InvalidateEventCacheCallback(Datum arg,
      54              :                                                                                  int cacheid, uint32 hashvalue);
      55              : static Bitmapset *DecodeTextArrayToBitmapset(Datum array);
      56              : 
      57              : /*
      58              :  * Search the event cache by trigger event.
      59              :  *
      60              :  * Note that the caller had better copy any data it wants to keep around
      61              :  * across any operation that might touch a system catalog into some other
      62              :  * memory context, since a cache reset could blow the return value away.
      63              :  */
      64              : List *
      65         4964 : EventCacheLookup(EventTriggerEvent event)
      66              : {
      67              :         EventTriggerCacheEntry *entry;
      68              : 
      69         4964 :         if (EventTriggerCacheState != ETCS_VALID)
      70            2 :                 BuildEventTriggerCache();
      71         4964 :         entry = hash_search(EventTriggerCache, &event, HASH_FIND, NULL);
      72         4964 :         return entry != NULL ? entry->triggerlist : NIL;
      73              : }
      74              : 
      75              : /*
      76              :  * Rebuild the event trigger cache.
      77              :  */
      78              : static void
      79            2 : BuildEventTriggerCache(void)
      80              : {
      81              :         HASHCTL         ctl;
      82              :         HTAB       *cache;
      83              :         MemoryContext oldcontext;
      84              :         Relation        rel;
      85              :         Relation        irel;
      86              :         SysScanDesc scan;
      87              : 
      88            2 :         if (EventTriggerCacheContext != NULL)
      89              :         {
      90              :                 /*
      91              :                  * Free up any memory already allocated in EventTriggerCacheContext.
      92              :                  * This can happen either because a previous rebuild failed, or
      93              :                  * because an invalidation happened before the rebuild was complete.
      94              :                  */
      95            0 :                 MemoryContextResetAndDeleteChildren(EventTriggerCacheContext);
      96              :         }
      97              :         else
      98              :         {
      99              :                 /*
     100              :                  * This is our first time attempting to build the cache, so we need to
     101              :                  * set up the memory context and register a syscache callback to
     102              :                  * capture future invalidation events.
     103              :                  */
     104            2 :                 if (CacheMemoryContext == NULL)
     105            0 :                         CreateCacheMemoryContext();
     106            2 :                 EventTriggerCacheContext =
     107            2 :                         AllocSetContextCreate(CacheMemoryContext,
     108              :                                                                   "EventTriggerCache",
     109              :                                                                   ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_SIZES);
     110            2 :                 CacheRegisterSyscacheCallback(EVENTTRIGGEROID,
     111              :                                                                           InvalidateEventCacheCallback,
     112              :                                                                           (Datum) 0);
     113              :         }
     114              : 
     115              :         /* Switch to correct memory context. */
     116            2 :         oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(EventTriggerCacheContext);
     117              : 
     118              :         /* Prevent the memory context from being nuked while we're rebuilding. */
     119            2 :         EventTriggerCacheState = ETCS_REBUILD_STARTED;
     120              : 
     121              :         /* Create new hash table. */
     122           28 :         MemSet(&ctl, 0, sizeof(ctl));
     123            2 :         ctl.keysize = sizeof(EventTriggerEvent);
     124            2 :         ctl.entrysize = sizeof(EventTriggerCacheEntry);
     125            2 :         ctl.hcxt = EventTriggerCacheContext;
     126            2 :         cache = hash_create("Event Trigger Cache", 32, &ctl,
     127              :                                                 HASH_ELEM | HASH_BLOBS | HASH_CONTEXT);
     128              : 
     129              :         /*
     130              :          * Prepare to scan pg_event_trigger in name order.
     131              :          */
     132            2 :         rel = relation_open(EventTriggerRelationId, AccessShareLock);
     133            2 :         irel = index_open(EventTriggerNameIndexId, AccessShareLock);
     134            2 :         scan = systable_beginscan_ordered(rel, irel, NULL, 0, NULL);
     135              : 
     136              :         /*
     137              :          * Build a cache item for each pg_event_trigger tuple, and append each one
     138              :          * to the appropriate cache entry.
     139              :          */
     140              :         for (;;)
     141            0 :         {
     142              :                 HeapTuple       tup;
     143              :                 Form_pg_event_trigger form;
     144              :                 char       *evtevent;
     145              :                 EventTriggerEvent event;
     146              :                 EventTriggerCacheItem *item;
     147              :                 Datum           evttags;
     148              :                 bool            evttags_isnull;
     149              :                 EventTriggerCacheEntry *entry;
     150              :                 bool            found;
     151              : 
     152              :                 /* Get next tuple. */
     153            2 :                 tup = systable_getnext_ordered(scan, ForwardScanDirection);
     154            2 :                 if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
     155            2 :                         break;
     156              : 
     157              :                 /* Skip trigger if disabled. */
     158            0 :                 form = (Form_pg_event_trigger) GETSTRUCT(tup);
     159            0 :                 if (form->evtenabled == TRIGGER_DISABLED)
     160            0 :                         continue;
     161              : 
     162              :                 /* Decode event name. */
     163            0 :                 evtevent = NameStr(form->evtevent);
     164            0 :                 if (strcmp(evtevent, "ddl_command_start") == 0)
     165            0 :                         event = EVT_DDLCommandStart;
     166            0 :                 else if (strcmp(evtevent, "ddl_command_end") == 0)
     167            0 :                         event = EVT_DDLCommandEnd;
     168            0 :                 else if (strcmp(evtevent, "sql_drop") == 0)
     169            0 :                         event = EVT_SQLDrop;
     170            0 :                 else if (strcmp(evtevent, "table_rewrite") == 0)
     171            0 :                         event = EVT_TableRewrite;
     172              :                 else
     173            0 :                         continue;
     174              : 
     175              :                 /* Allocate new cache item. */
     176            0 :                 item = palloc0(sizeof(EventTriggerCacheItem));
     177            0 :                 item->fnoid = form->evtfoid;
     178            0 :                 item->enabled = form->evtenabled;
     179              : 
     180              :                 /* Decode and sort tags array. */
     181            0 :                 evttags = heap_getattr(tup, Anum_pg_event_trigger_evttags,
     182              :                                                            RelationGetDescr(rel), &evttags_isnull);
     183            0 :                 if (!evttags_isnull)
     184            0 :                         item->tagset = DecodeTextArrayToBitmapset(evttags);
     185              : 
     186              :                 /* Add to cache entry. */
     187            0 :                 entry = hash_search(cache, &event, HASH_ENTER, &found);
     188            0 :                 if (found)
     189            0 :                         entry->triggerlist = lappend(entry->triggerlist, item);
     190              :                 else
     191            0 :                         entry->triggerlist = list_make1(item);
     192              :         }
     193              : 
     194              :         /* Done with pg_event_trigger scan. */
     195            2 :         systable_endscan_ordered(scan);
     196            2 :         index_close(irel, AccessShareLock);
     197            2 :         relation_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
     198              : 
     199              :         /* Restore previous memory context. */
     200            2 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
     201              : 
     202              :         /* Install new cache. */
     203            2 :         EventTriggerCache = cache;
     204              : 
     205              :         /*
     206              :          * If the cache has been invalidated since we entered this routine, we
     207              :          * still use and return the cache we just finished constructing, to avoid
     208              :          * infinite loops, but we leave the cache marked stale so that we'll
     209              :          * rebuild it again on next access.  Otherwise, we mark the cache valid.
     210              :          */
     211            2 :         if (EventTriggerCacheState == ETCS_REBUILD_STARTED)
     212            2 :                 EventTriggerCacheState = ETCS_VALID;
     213            2 : }
     214              : 
     215              : /*
     216              :  * Decode text[] to a Bitmapset of CommandTags.
     217              :  *
     218              :  * We could avoid a bit of overhead here if we were willing to duplicate some
     219              :  * of the logic from deconstruct_array, but it doesn't seem worth the code
     220              :  * complexity.
     221              :  */
     222              : static Bitmapset *
     223            0 : DecodeTextArrayToBitmapset(Datum array)
     224              : {
     225            0 :         ArrayType  *arr = DatumGetArrayTypeP(array);
     226              :         Datum      *elems;
     227              :         Bitmapset  *bms;
     228              :         int                     i;
     229              :         int                     nelems;
     230              : 
     231            0 :         if (ARR_NDIM(arr) != 1 || ARR_HASNULL(arr) || ARR_ELEMTYPE(arr) != TEXTOID)
     232            0 :                 elog(ERROR, "expected 1-D text array");
     233            0 :         deconstruct_array(arr, TEXTOID, -1, false, TYPALIGN_INT,
     234              :                                           &elems, NULL, &nelems);
     235              : 
     236            0 :         for (bms = NULL, i = 0; i < nelems; ++i)
     237              :         {
     238            0 :                 char       *str = TextDatumGetCString(elems[i]);
     239              : 
     240            0 :                 bms = bms_add_member(bms, GetCommandTagEnum(str));
     241            0 :                 pfree(str);
     242              :         }
     243              : 
     244            0 :         pfree(elems);
     245              : 
     246            0 :         return bms;
     247              : }
     248              : 
     249              : /*
     250              :  * Flush all cache entries when pg_event_trigger is updated.
     251              :  *
     252              :  * This should be rare enough that we don't need to be very granular about
     253              :  * it, so we just blow away everything, which also avoids the possibility of
     254              :  * memory leaks.
     255              :  */
     256              : static void
     257            0 : InvalidateEventCacheCallback(Datum arg, int cacheid, uint32 hashvalue)
     258              : {
     259              :         /*
     260              :          * If the cache isn't valid, then there might be a rebuild in progress, so
     261              :          * we can't immediately blow it away.  But it's advantageous to do this
     262              :          * when possible, so as to immediately free memory.
     263              :          */
     264            0 :         if (EventTriggerCacheState == ETCS_VALID)
     265              :         {
     266            0 :                 MemoryContextResetAndDeleteChildren(EventTriggerCacheContext);
     267            0 :                 EventTriggerCache = NULL;
     268              :         }
     269              : 
     270              :         /* Mark cache for rebuild. */
     271            0 :         EventTriggerCacheState = ETCS_NEEDS_REBUILD;
     272            0 : }
